* [BUGFIX] Fix draining of file watcher events in file-based service discovery.\\n2670: * [BUGFIX] Add `POST` handler for `/debug` endpoints to fix CPU profiling.\\n2671: * [BUGFIX] Fix several flaky tests.\\n2672: \",\"endLine\":2672,\"startLine\":2669},{\"content\":\"2507: * [FEATURE] Add Graphite remote-storage integration\\n2508: * [FEATURE] Create separate Kubernetes targets for services and their endpoints\\n2509: * [FEATURE] Add `clamp_{min,max}` functions to PromQL\\n2510: \",\"endLine\":2510,\"startLine\":2507},{\"content\":\"208: * [CHANGE] Logging: the format of log lines has changed a little, along with the adoption of Go's Structured Logging package. #14906\\n209: * [CHANGE] Don't create extra `_created` timeseries if feature-flag `created-timestamp-zero-ingestion` is enabled. #14738\\n210: * [CHANGE] Float literals and time durations being the same is now a stable fetaure. #15111\\n211: statsRenderer\\n331: \\t}\\n332: \\n333: \\tif ap == nil && (rwEnabled || otlpEnabled) {\\n334: \\t\\tpanic(\\\"remote write or otlp write enabled, but no appender passed in.\\\")\\n335: \\t}\\n336: \\n337: \\tif rwEnabled {\\n338: \\t\\ta.remoteWriteHandler = remote.NewWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, acceptRemoteWriteProtoMsgs, ctZeroIngestionEnabled)\\n339: \\t}\\n340: \\tif otlpEnabled {\\n341: \\t\\ta.otlpWriteHandler = remote.NewOTLPWriteHandler(logger, registerer, ap, configFunc, remote.OTLPOptions{\\n342: \\t\\t\\tConvertDelta:            otlpDeltaToCumulative,\\n343: \\t\\t\\tNativeDelta:             otlpNativeDeltaIngestion,\\n344: \\t\\t\\tLookbackDelta:           lookbackDelta,\\n345: \\t\\t\\tEnableTypeAndUnitLabels: enableTypeAndUnitLabels,\\n346: \\t\\t})\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\n349: \\treturn a\\n350: }\\n351: \\n352: // InstallCodec adds codec to this API's available codecs.\\n353: // Codecs installed first take precedence over codecs installed later when evaluating wildcards in Accept headers.\\n354: // The first installed codec is used as a fallback when the Accept header cannot be satisfied or if there is no Accept header.\\n355: func (api *API) InstallCodec(codec Codec) {\\n356: \\tapi.codecs = append(api.codecs, codec)\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: // ClearCodecs removes all available codecs from this API, including the default codec installed by NewAPI.\\n360: func (api *API) ClearCodecs() {\\n361: \\tapi.codecs = nil\\n362: }\\n363: \\n364: func setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(r apiFuncResult) apiFuncResult {\\n365: \\tif r.err != nil && errors.Is(r.err.err, tsdb.ErrNotReady) {\\n366: \\t\\tr.err.typ = errorUnavailable\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn r\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\\n372: func (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\\n373: \\twrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\\n374: \\t\\thf := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n375: \\t\\t\\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\\n376: \\t\\t\\tresult := setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(f(r))\\n377: \\t\\t\\tif result.finalizer != nil {\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefer result.finalizer()\\n379: \\t\\t\\t}\\n380: \\t\\t\\tif result.err != nil {\\n381: \\t\\t\\t\\tapi.respondError(w, result.err, result.data)\\n382: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n383: \\t\\t\\t}\\n384: \\n385: \\t\\t\\tif result.data != nil {\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tapi.respond(w, r, result.data, result.warnings, r.FormValue(\\\"query\\\"))\\n387: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n388: \\t\\t\\t}\\n389: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\\n390: \\t\\t})\\n391: \\t\\treturn api.ready(httputil.CompressionHandler{\\n392: \\t\\t\\tHandler: hf,\\n393: \\t\\t}.ServeHTTP)\\n394: default stats renderer for the API.\\n150: func DefaultStatsRenderer(_ context.Context, s *stats.Statistics, param string) stats.QueryStats {\\n151: \\tif param != \\\"\\\" {\\n152: \\t\\treturn stats.NewQueryStats(s)\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\treturn nil\\n155: }\\n156: \\n157: // PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\\n158: type PrometheusVersion struct {\\n159: \\t\\\"path\\\"\\n20: \\n21: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery/targetgroup\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/relabel\\\"\\n27: )\\n28: \\n29: // Alertmanager holds Alertmanager endpoint information.\\n30: type alertmanager interface {\\n31: \\turl() *url.URL\\n32: }\\n33: \\n34: type alertmanagerLabels struct{ labels.Labels }\\n35: \\n36: const pathLabel = \\\"__alerts_path__\\\"\\n37: \\n38: func (a alertmanagerLabels) url() *url.URL {\\n39: \\treturn &url.URL{\\n40: \\t\\tScheme: a.Get(model.SchemeLabel),\\n41: \\t\\tHost:   a.Get(model.AddressLabel),\\n42: \\t\\tPath:   a.Get(pathLabel),\\n43: \\t}\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: // AlertmanagerFromGroup extracts a list of alertmanagers from a target group\\n47: // and an associated AlertmanagerConfig.\\n48: func AlertmanagerFromGroup(tg *targetgroup.Group, cfg *config.AlertmanagerConfig) ([]alertmanager, []alertmanager, error) {\\n49: \\tvar res []alertmanager\\n50: \\tvar droppedAlertManagers []alertmanager\\n51: \\tlb := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\\n52: \\n53: \\tfor _, tlset := range tg.Targets {\\n54: \\t\\tlb.Reset(labels.EmptyLabels())\\n55: \\n56: \\t\\tfor ln, lv := range tlset {\\n57: \\t\\t\\tlb.Set(string(ln), string(lv))\\n58: \\t\\t}\\n59: \\t\\t// Set configured scheme as the initial scheme label for overwrite.\\n60: \\t\\tlb.Set(model.SchemeLabel, cfg.Scheme)\\n61: \\t\\tlb.Set(pathLabel, postPath(cfg.PathPrefix, cfg.APIVersion))\\n62: \\n63: \\t\\t// Combine target labels with target group labels.\\n64: \\t\\tfor ln, lv := range tg.Labels {\\n65: \\t\\t\\tif _, ok := tlset[ln]; !ok {\\n66: \\t\\t\\t\\tlb.Set(string(ln), string(lv))\\n67: \\t\\t\\t}\\n68: \\t\\t}\\n69: \\n70: \\t\\tpreRelabel := lb.Labels()\\n71: \\t\\tkeep := relabel.ProcessBuilder(lb, cfg.RelabelConfigs...)\\n72: \\t\\tif !keep {\\n73: \\t\\t\\tdroppedAlertManagers = append(droppedAlertManagers, alertmanagerLabels{preRelabel})\\n74: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n75: \\t\\t}\\n76: \\n77: \\t\\taddr := lb.Get(model.AddressLabel)\\n78: \\t\\tif err := config.CheckTargetAddress(model.LabelValue(addr)); err != nil {\\n79: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n80: \\t\\t}\\n81: \\n82: \\t\\tres = append(res, alertmanagerLabels{lb.Labels()})\\n83: \\t}\\n84: \\treturn res, droppedAlertManagers, nil\\n85: }\\n86: \\n87: func postPath(pre string, v config.AlertmanagerAPIVersion) string {\\n88: \\talertPushEndpoint := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/api/%v/alerts\\\", string(v))\\n89: \\treturn path.Join(\\\"/\\\", pre, alertPushEndpoint)\\n90: }\\n91: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/notifier/alertmanager.go\"}"

\\tMin     int `json:\\\"min\\\"`\\n1832: \\tMax     int `json:\\\"max\\\"`\\n1833: \\tCurrent int `json:\\\"current\\\"`\\n1834: }\\n1835: \\n1836: func (api *API) serveWALReplayStatus(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n1837: \\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\\n1838: \\tstatus, err := api.db.WALReplayStatus()\\n1839: \\tif err != nil {\\n1840: \\t\\tapi.respondError(w, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil)\\n1841: \\t}\\n1842: \\tapi.respond(w, r, walReplayStatus{\\n1843: \\t\\tMin:     status.Min,\\n1844: \\t\\tMax:     status.Max,\\n1845: \\t\\tCurrent: status.Current,\\n1846: \\t}, nil, \\\"\\\")\\n1847: }\\n1848: \\n1849: func (api *API) notifications(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n1850: \\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\\n1851: \\tapi.respond(w, r, api.notificationsGetter(), nil, \\\"\\\")\\n1852: }\\n1853: \\n1854: func (api *API) notificationsSSE(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n1855: \\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\\n1856: \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/event-stream\\\")\\n1857: \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Cache-Control\\\", \\\"no-cache\\\")\\n1858: \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Connection\\\", \\\"keep-alive\\\")\\n1859: \\n1860: \\t// Subscribe to notifications.\\n1861: \\tnotifications, unsubscribe, ok := api.notificationsSub()\\n1862: \\tif !ok {\\n1863: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\\n1864: \\t\\treturn\\n1865: \\t}\\n1866: \\tdefer unsubscribe()\\n1867: \\n1868: \\t// Set up a flusher to push the response to the client.\\n1869: \\tflusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)\\n1870: \\tif !ok {\\n1871: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, \\\"Streaming unsupported\\\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n1872: \\t\\treturn\\n1873: \\t}\\n1874: \\n1875: \\t// Flush the response to ensure the headers are immediately and eventSource\\n1876: \\t// onopen is triggered client-side.\\n1877: \\tflusher.Flush()\\n1878: \\n1879: \\tfor {\\n1880: \\t\\tselect {\\n1881: \\t\\tcase notification := <-notifications:\\n1882: \\t\\t\\t// Marshal the notification to JSON.\\n1883: \\t\\t\\tjsonData, err := json.Marshal(notification)\\n1884: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1885: \\t\\t\\t\\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n1886: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n1887: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1888: \\n1889: \\t\\t\\t// Write the event data in SSE format with JSON content.\\n1890: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(w, \\\"data: %s\\\\n\\\\n\\\", jsonData)\\n1891: \\n1892: \\t\\t\\t// Flush the response to ensure the data is sent immediately.\\n1893: \\t\\t\\tflusher.Flush()\\n1894: \\t\\tcase <-r.Context().Done():\\n1895: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n1896: \\t\\t}\\n1897: \\t}\\n1898: }\\n1899: \\n1900: func (api *API) remoteRead(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n1901: \\t// This is only really for tests - this will never be nil IRL.\\n1902: \\tif api.remoteReadHandler != nil {\\n1903: \\t\\tapi.remoteReadHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n1904: \\t} else {\\n1905: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, \\\"not found\\\", http.StatusNotFound)\\n1906: \\t}\\n1907: }\\n1908: \\n1909: func (api *API) remoteWrite(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n1910: \\tif api.remoteWriteHandler != nil {\\n1911: \\t\\tapi.remoteWriteHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n1912: \\t} else {\\n1913: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, \\\"remote write receiver needs to be enabled with --web.enable-remote-write-receiver\\\", http.StatusNotFound)\\n1914: \\t}\\n1915: }\\n1916: \\n1917: func (api *API) otlpWrite(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n1918: \\tif api.otlpWriteHandler != nil {\\n1919: \\t\\tapi.otlpWriteHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n1920: \\t} else {\\n1921: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, \\\"otlp write receiver needs to be enabled with --web.enable-otlp-receiver\\\", http.StatusNotFound)\\n1922: \\t}\\n1923: }\\n1924: \\n1925: func (api *API) deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n1926: \\tif !api.enableAdmin {\\n1927: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\\\"admin APIs disabled\\\")}, nil, nil}\\n1928: \\t}\\n1929: \\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\\n1930: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error parsing form values: %w\\\", err)}, nil, nil}\\n1931: \\t}\\n1932: \\tif len(r.Form[\\\"match[]\\\"]) == 0 {\\n1933: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\\\"no match[] parameter provided\\\")}, nil, nil}\\n1934: \\t}\\n1935: \\n1936: \\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"start\\\", MinTime)\\n1937: \\tif err != nil {\\n1938: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"start\\\")\\n1939: \\t}\\n1940: \\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \\\"end\\\", MaxTime)\\n1941: \\tif err != nil {\\n1942: \\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"end\\\")\\n1943: \\t}\\n1944: \\n1945: \\tfor _, s := range r.Form[\\\"match[]\\\"] {\\n1946: \\t\\tmatchers, err := parser.ParseMetricSelector(s)\\n1947: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1948: \\t\\t\\treturn invalidParamError(err, \\\"match[]\\\")\\n1949: \\t\\t}\\n1950: \\t\\tif err := api.db.Delete(r.Context(), timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), matchers...); err != nil {\\n1951: \\t\\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\\n1952: \\t\\t}\\n1953: \\t}\\n1954: \\n1955: \\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\\n1956: }\\n1957: \\n1958: func (api *API) snapshot(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\\n1959: \\tif !api.enableAdmin {\\n1960: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\\\"admin APIs disabled\\\")}, nil, nil}\\n1961: \\t}\\n1962: \\tvar (\\n1963: \\t\\tskipHead bool\\n1964: \\t\\terr      error\\n1965: \\t)\\n1966: \\tif r.FormValue(\\\"skip_head\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n1967: \\t\\tskipHead, err = strconv.ParseBool(r.FormValue(\\\"skip_head\\\"))\\n1968: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n1969: \\t\\t\\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to parse boolean: %w\\\", err), \\\"skip_head\\\")\\n1970: \\t\\t}\\n1971: \\t}\\n1972: \\n1973: \\tvar (\\n1974: \\t\\tsnapdir = filepath.Join(api.dbDir, \\\"snapshots\\\")\\n1975: \\t\\tname    = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s-%016x\\\",\\n1976: \\t\\t\\ttime.Now().UTC().Format(\\\"20060102T150405Z0700\\\"),\\n1977: \\t\\t\\trand.Int63())\\n1978: \\t\\tdir = filepath.Join(snapdir, name)\\n1979: \\t)\\n1980: \\tif err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o777); err != nil {\\n1981: \\t\\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\\\"create snapshot err != nil {\\n2032: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error writing response\\\", \\\"url\\\", req.URL, \\\"bytesWritten\\\", n, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2033: \\t}\\n2034: }\\n2035: \\n2036: func (api *API) negotiateCodec(req *http.Request, resp *Response) (Codec, error) {\\n2037: \\tfor _, clause := range goautoneg.ParseAccept(req.Header.Get(\\\"Accept\\\")) {\\n2038: \\t\\tfor _, codec := range api.codecs {\\n2039: \\t\\t\\tif codec.ContentType().Satisfies(clause) && codec.CanEncode(resp) {\\n2040: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn codec, nil\\n2041: \\t\\t\\t}\\n2042: \\t\\t}\\n2043: \\t}\\n2044: \\n2045: \\tdefaultCodec := api.codecs[0]\\n2046: \\tif !defaultCodec.CanEncode(resp) {\\n2047: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot encode response as %s\\\", defaultCodec.ContentType())\\n2048: \\t}\\n2049: \\n2050: \\treturn defaultCodec, nil\\n2051: }\\n2052: \\n2053: func (api *API) respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, apiErr *apiError, data any) {\\n2054: \\tjson := jsoniter.ConfigCompatibleWithStandardLibrary\\n2055: \\tb, err := json.Marshal(&Response{\\n2056: \\t\\tStatus:    statusError,\\n2057: \\t\\tErrorType: apiErr.typ.str,\\n2058: \\t\\tError:     apiErr.err.Error(),\\n2059: \\t\\tData:      data,\\n2060: \\t})\\n2061: \\tif err != nil {\\n2062: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error marshaling json response\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2063: \\t\\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n2064: \\t\\treturn\\n2065: \\t}\\n2066: \\n2067: \\tvar code int\\n2068: \\tif api.overrideErrorCode != nil {\\n2069: \\t\\tif newCode, override := api.overrideErrorCode(apiErr.typ.num, apiErr.err); override {\\n2070: \\t\\t\\tcode = newCode\\n2071: \\t\\t} else {\\n2072: \\t\\t\\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\\n2073: \\t\\t}\\n2074: \\t} else {\\n2075: \\t\\tcode = getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ)\\n2076: \\t}\\n2077: \\n2078: \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n2079: \\tw.WriteHeader(code)\\n2080: \\tif n, err := w.Write(b); err != nil {\\n2081: \\t\\tapi.logger.Error(\\\"error writing response\\\", \\\"bytesWritten\\\", n, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n2082: \\t}\\n2083: }\\n2084: \\n2085: func getDefaultErrorCode(errType errorType) int {\\n2086: \\tswitch errType {\\n2087: \\tcase errorBadData:\\n2088: \\t\\treturn http.StatusBadRequest\\n2089: \\tcase errorExec:\\n2090: \\t\\treturn http.StatusUnprocessableEntity\\n2091: \\tcase errorCanceled:\\n2092: \\t\\treturn statusClientClosedConnection\\n2093: \\tcase errorTimeout:\\n2094: \\t\\treturn http.StatusServiceUnavailable\\n2095: \\tcase errorInternal:\\n2096: \\t\\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\\n2097: \\tcase errorNotFound:\\n2098: \\t\\treturn http.StatusNotFound\\n2099: \\tcase errorNotAcceptable:\\n2100: \\t\\treturn http.StatusNotAcceptable\\n2101: \\tdefault:\\n2102: \\t\\treturn http.StatusInternalServerError\\n2103: \\t}\\n2104: }\\n2105: \\n2106: func parseTimeParam(r *http.Request, paramName string, defaultValue time.Time) (time.Time, error) {\\n2107: \\tval := r.FormValue(paramName)\\n2108: \\tif val == \\\"\\\" {\\n2109: \\t\\treturn defaultValue, nil\\n2110: \\t}\\n2111: \\tresult, err := parseTime(val)\\n2112: \\tif err != nil {\\n2113: \\t\\treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid time value for '%s': %w\\\", paramName, err)\\n2114: \\t}\\n2115: \\treturn result, nil\\n2116: }\\n2117: \\n2118: func parseTime(s string) (time.Time, error) {\\n2119: \\tif t, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\\n2120: \\t\\ts, ns := math.Modf(t)\\n2121: \\t\\tns = math.Round(ns*1000) / 1000\\n2122: \\t\\treturn time.Unix(int64(s), int64(ns*float64(time.Second))).UTC(), nil\\n2123: \\t}\\n2124: \\tif t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s); err == nil {\\n2125: \\t\\treturn t, nil\\n2126: \\t}\\n2127: \\n2128: \\t// Stdlib's time parser can only handle 4 digit years. As a workaround until\\n2129: \\t// that is fixed we want to at least support our own boundary times.\\n2130: \\t// Context: https://github.com/prometheus/client_golang/issues/614\\n2131: \\t// Upstream issue: https://github.com/golang/go/issues/20555\\n2132: \\tswitch s {\\n2133: \\tcase minTimeFormatted:\\n2134: \\t\\treturn MinTime, nil\\n2135: \\tcase maxTimeFormatted:\\n2136: \\t\\treturn MaxTime, nil\\n2137: \\t}\\n2138: \\treturn time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot parse %q to a valid timestamp\\\", s)\\n2139: }\\n2140: \\n2141: func parseDuration(s string) (time.Duration, error) {\\n2142: \\tif d, err := strconv.ParseFloat(s, 64); err == nil {\\n2143: \\t\\tts := d * float64(time.Second)\\n2144: \\t\\tif ts > float64(math.MaxInt64) || ts < float64(math.MinInt64) {\\n2145: \\t\\t\\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration. It overflows int64\\\", s)\\n2146: \\t\\t}\\n2147: \\t\\treturn time.Duration(ts), nil\\n2148: \\t}\\n2149: \\tif d, err := model.ParseDuration(s); err == nil {\\n2150: \\t\\treturn time.Duration(d), nil\\n2151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/web/api/v1/api.go%3FL2000-2150\"}"

the specific language governing permissions and\\n12: // limitations under the License.\\n13: \\n14: package notifier\\n15: \\n16: import (\\n17: \\t\\\"crypto/md5\\\"\\n18: \\t\\\"encoding/hex\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"log/slog\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\tconfig_util \\\"github.com/prometheus/common/config\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/sigv4\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"gopkg.in/yaml.v2\\\"\\n26: \\n27: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/discovery/targetgroup\\\"\\n29: )\\n30: \\n31: // alertmanagerSet contains a set of Alertmanagers discovered via a group of service\\n32: // discovery definitions that have a common configuration on how alerts should be sent.\\n33: type alertmanagerSet struct {\\n34: \\tcfg    *config.AlertmanagerConfig\\n35: \\tclient *http.Client\\n36: \\n37: \\tmetrics *alertMetrics\\n38: \\n39: \\tmtx        sync.RWMutex\\n40: \\tams        []alertmanager\\n41: \\tdroppedAms []alertmanager\\n42: \\tlogger     *slog.Logger\\n43: }\\n44: \\n45: func newAlertmanagerSet(cfg *config.AlertmanagerConfig, logger *slog.Logger, metrics *alertMetrics) (*alertmanagerSet, error) {\\n46: \\tclient, err := config_util.NewClientFromConfig(cfg.HTTPClientConfig, \\\"alertmanager\\\")\\n47: \\tif err != nil {\\n48: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n49: \\t}\\n50: \\tt := client.Transport\\n51: \\n52: \\tif cfg.SigV4Config != nil {\\n53: \\t\\tt, err = sigv4.NewSigV4RoundTripper(cfg.SigV4Config, client.Transport)\\n54: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n55: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n56: \\t\\t}\\n57: \\t}\\n58: \\n59: \\tclient.Transport = t\\n60: \\n61: \\ts := &alertmanagerSet{\\n62: \\t\\tclient:  client,\\n63: \\t\\tcfg:     cfg,\\n64: \\t\\tlogger:  logger,\\n65: \\t\\tmetrics: metrics,\\n66: \\t}\\n67: \\treturn s, nil\\n68: }\\n69: \\n70: // sync extracts a deduplicated set of Alertmanager endpoints from a list\\n71: // of target groups definitions.\\n72: func (s *alertmanagerSet) sync(tgs []*targetgroup.Group) {\\n73: \\tallAms := []alertmanager{}\\n74: \\tallDroppedAms := []alertmanager{}\\n75: \\n76: \\tfor _, tg := range tgs {\\n77: \\t\\tams, droppedAms, err := AlertmanagerFromGroup(tg, s.cfg)\\n78: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n79: \\t\\t\\ts.logger.Error(\\\"Creating discovered Alertmanagers failed\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n80: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n81: \\t\\t}\\n82: \\t\\tallAms = append(allAms, ams...)\\n83: \\t\\tallDroppedAms = append(allDroppedAms, droppedAms...)\\n84: \\t}\\n85: \\n86: \\ts.mtx.Lock()\\n87: \\tdefer s.mtx.Unlock()\\n88: \\tpreviousAms := s.ams\\n89: \\t// Set new Alertmanagers and deduplicate them along their unique URL.\\n90: \\ts.ams = []alertmanager{}\\n91: \\ts.droppedAms = []alertmanager{}\\n92: \\ts.droppedAms = append(s.droppedAms, allDroppedAms...)\\n93: \\tseen := map[string]struct{}{}\\n94: \\n95: \\tfor _, am := range allAms {\\n96: \\t\\tus := am.url().String()\\n97: \\t\\tif _, ok := seen[us]; ok {\\n98: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n99: \\t\\t}\\n100: \\n101: \\t\\t// This will initialize the Counters for the AM to 0.\\n102: \\t\\ts.metrics.sent.WithLabelValues(us)\\n103: \\t\\ts.metrics.errors.WithLabelValues(us)\\n104: \\n105: \\t\\tseen[us] = struct{}{}\\n106: \\t\\ts.ams = append(s.ams, am)\\n107: \\t}\\n108: \\t// Now remove counters for any removed Alertmanagers.\\n109: \\tfor _, am := range previousAms {\\n110: \\t\\tus := am.url().String()\\n111: \\t\\tif _, ok := seen[us]; ok {\\n112: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n113: \\t\\t}\\n114: \\t\\ts.metrics.latency.DeleteLabelValues(us)\\n115: \\t\\ts.metrics.sent.DeleteLabelValues(us)\\n116: \\t\\ts.metrics.errors.DeleteLabelValues(us)\\n117: \\t\\tseen[us] = struct{}{}\\n118: \\t}\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func (s *alertmanagerSet) configHash() (string, error) {\\n122: \\tb, err := yaml.Marshal(s.cfg)\\n123: \\tif err != nil {\\n124: \\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n125: \\t}\\n126: \\thash := md5.Sum(b)\\n127: \\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]), nil\\n128: }\\n129: \\n2767: ### `<alertmanager_config>`\\n2768: \\n2769: An `alertmanager_config` section specifies Alertmanager instances the Prometheus\\n2770: server sends alerts to. It also provides parameters to configure how to\\n2771: communicate with these Alertmanagers.\\n2772: \\n2773: Alertmanagers may be statically configured via the `static_configs` parameter or\\n2774: dynamically discovered using one of the supported service-discovery mechanisms.\\n2775: \",\"endLine\":2775,\"startLine\":2766},{\"content\":\"173:     [ - <relabel_config> ... ]\\n174:   alertmanagers:\\n175:     [ - <alertmanager_config> ... ]\\n176: \\n177: \",\"endLine\":177,\"startLine\":173},{\"content\":\"690: ```yaml\\n691: client_id: <string>\\n692: [ client_secret: <secret> ]\\n693: \\n694: # Read the client secret from a file.\\n695: # It is mutually exclusive with `client_secret`.\\n696: [ client_secret_file: <filename> ]\\n697: \\n698: \",\"endLine\":698,\"startLine\":690}],\"file\":\"docs/configuration/configuration.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/configuration/configuration.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"240: \\t\\n241: \\tHTTPClientOptions []config.HTTPClientOption\\n242: }\\n243: \",\"endLine\":243,\"startLine\":240},{\"content\":\"263: - List the service discovery in both `<scrape_config>` and\\n264:   `<alertmanager_config>` in `docs/configuration/configuration.md`.\\n265: \\n266: \",\"endLine\":266,\"startLine\":263},{\"content\":\"246: It's used to tag the provided `Logger` and as the part of the YAML key for your SD\\n247: mechanism's list of configs in `scrape_config` and `alertmanager_config`\\n248: (e.g. `${NAME}_sd_configs`).\\n249: \",\"endLine\":249,\"startLine\":246}],\"file\":\"discovery/README.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/discovery/README.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"51: | <code class=\\\"text-nowrap\\\">--rules.alert.for-grace-period</code> | Minimum duration between alert and restored \\\"for\\\" state. This is maintained only for alerts with configured \\\"for\\\" time greater than grace period. Use with server mode only. | `10m` |\\n52: | <code class=\\\"text-nowrap\\\">--rules.alert.resend-delay</code> | Minimum amount of time to wait before resending an alert to Alertmanager. Use with server mode only. | `1m` |\\n53: | <code class=\\\"text-nowrap\\\">--rules.max-concurrent-evals</code> | Global concurrency limit for independent rules that can run concurrently. When set, \\\"query.max-concurrency\\\" may need to be adjusted accordingly. Use with server mode only. | `4` |\\n54: | <code class=\\\"text-nowrap\\\">--alertmanager.notification-queue-capacity</code> | The capacity of the queue for pending Alertmanager notifications. Use with server mode only. | `10000` |\\n55: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":58,\"startLine\":51},{\"content\":\"48: | <code class=\\\"text-nowrap\\\">--storage.remote.read-concurrent-limit</code> | Maximum number of concurrent remote read calls. 0 means no limit. Use with server mode only. | `10` |\\n49: | <code class=\\\"text-nowrap\\\">--storage.remote.read-max-bytes-in-frame</code> | Maximum number of bytes in a single frame for streaming remote read response types before marshalling. Note that client might have limit on frame size as well. 1MB as recommended ... [line truncated]\\n50: | <code class=\\\"text-nowrap\\\">--rules.alert.for-outage-tolerance</code> | Max time to tolerate prometheus outage for restoring \\\"for\\\" state of alert. Use with server mode only. | `1h` |\\n51: \",\"endLine\":51,\"startLine\":48}],\"file\":\"docs/command-line/prometheus.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/command-line/prometheus.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"236: \\n237: ### Disallow configuring Alertmanager with the v1 API\\n238: Prometheus 3 no longer supports Alertmanager's v1 API. Effectively Prometheus 3\\n239: requires [Alertmanager 0.16.0](https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/releases/tag/v0.16.0) or later. Users with older Alertmanager\\n240: versions or configurations that use `alerting: alertmanagers: [api_version: v1]`\\n241: need to upgrade Alertmanager and change their configuration to use `api_version: v2`.\\n242: \\n243: \",\"endLine\":243,\"startLine\":236},{\"content\":\"54: - The `http_config.enable_http2` in `remote_write` items default has been\\n55:   changed to `false`. In Prometheus v2 the remote write http client would\\n56:   default to use http2. In order to parallelize multiple remote write queues\\n57: \",\"endLine\":57,\"startLine\":54}],\"file\":\"docs/migration.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/migration.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2126: \\n2127: * [BUGFIX] Fix disappearing Alertmanager targets in Alertmanager discovery.\\n2128: * [BUGFIX] Fix panic with remote_write on ARMv7.\\n2129: \",\"endLine\":2129,\"startLine\":2126},{\"content\":\"1227: \\n1228: In the next minor release (2.26), Prometheus will use the Alertmanager API v2.\\n1229: It will be done by defaulting `alertmanager_config.api_version` to `v2`.\\n1230: Alertmanager API v2 was released in Alertmanager v0.16.0 (released in January\\n1231: 2019).\\n1232: \",\"endLine\":1232,\"startLine\":1227},{\"content\":\"2464: * [ENHANCEMENT] Quarantine series file if data corruption is encountered (instead of crashing)\\n2465: * [ENHANCEMENT] Validate Alertmanager URL\\n2466: * [ENHANCEMENT] Use UTC for build timestamp\\n2467: \",\"endLine\":2467,\"startLine\":2464}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/CHANGELOG.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"40:       {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2766: \n2767: ### `<alertmanager_config>`\n2768: \n2769: An `alertmanager_config` section specifies Alertmanager instances the Prometheus\n2770: server sends alerts to. It also provides parameters to configure how to\n2771: communicate with these Alertmanagers.\n2772: \n2773: Alertmanagers may be statically configured via the `static_configs` parameter or\n2774: dynamically discovered using one of the supported service-discovery mechanisms.\n2775: ",
            "endLine": 2775,
            "startLine": 2766
          },
          {
            "content": "173:     [ - <relabel_config> ... ]\n174:   alertmanagers:\n175:     [ - <alertmanager_config> ... ]\n176: \n177: ",
            "endLine": 177,
            "startLine": 173
          },
          {
            "content": "690: ```yaml\n691: client_id: <string>\n692: [ client_secret: <secret> ]\n693: \n694: # Read the client secret from a file.\n695: # It is mutually exclusive with `client_secret`.\n696: [ client_secret_file: <filename> ]\n697: \n698: ",
            "endLine": 698,
            "startLine": 690
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/configuration/configuration.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/configuration/configuration.md"
      },

          {
            "content": "240: \t\n241: \tHTTPClientOptions []config.HTTPClientOption\n242: }\n243: ",
            "endLine": 243,
            "startLine": 240
          },
          {
            "content": "263: - List the service discovery in both `<scrape_config>` and\n264:   `<alertmanager_config>` in `docs/configuration/configuration.md`.\n265: \n266: ",
            "endLine": 266,
            "startLine": 263

          {
            "content": "246: It's used to tag the provided `Logger` and as the part of the YAML key for your SD\n247: mechanism's list of configs in `scrape_config` and `alertmanager_config`\n248: (e.g. `${NAME}_sd_configs`).\n249: ",

          {
            "content": "51: | <code class=\"text-nowrap\">--rules.alert.for-grace-period</code> | Minimum duration between alert and restored \"for\" state. This is maintained only for alerts with configured \"for\" time greater than grace period. Use with server mode only. | `10m` |\n52: | <code class=\"text-nowrap\">--rules.alert.resend-delay</code> | Minimum amount of time to wait before resending an alert to Alertmanager. Use with server mode only. | `1m` |\n53: | <code class=\"text-nowrap\">--rules.max-concurrent-evals</code> | Global concurrency limit for independent rules that can run concurrently. When set, \"query.max-concurrency\" may need to be adjusted accordingly. Use with server mode only. | `4` |\n54: | <code class=\"text-nowrap\">--alertmanager.notification-queue-capacity</code> | The capacity of the queue for pending Alertmanager notifications. Use with server mode only. | `10000` |\n55: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 58,
            "startLine": 51
          },
          {
            "content": "48: | <code class=\"text-nowrap\">--storage.remote.read-concurrent-limit</code> | Maximum number of concurrent remote read calls. 0 means no limit. Use with server mode only. | `10` |\n49: | <code class=\"text-nowrap\">--storage.remote.read-max-bytes-in-frame</code> | Maximum number of bytes in a single frame for streaming remote read response types before marshalling. Note that client might have limit on frame size as well. 1MB as recommended ... [line truncated]\n50: | <code class=\"text-nowrap\">--rules.alert.for-outage-tolerance</code> | Max time to tolerate prometheus outage for restoring \"for\" state of alert. Use with server mode only. | `1h` |\n51: ",
            "endLine": 51,
            "startLine": 48
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/command-line/prometheus.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/command-line/prometheus.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "236: \n237: ### Disallow configuring Alertmanager with the v1 API\n238: Prometheus 3 no longer supports Alertmanager's v1 API. Effectively Prometheus 3\n239: requires [Alertmanager 0.16.0](https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/releases/tag/v0.16.0) or later. Users with older Alertmanager\n240: versions or configurations that use `alerting: alertmanagers: [api_version: v1]`\n241: need to upgrade Alertmanager and change their configuration to use `api_version: v2`.\n242: \n243: ",
            "endLine": 243,
            "startLine": 236
          },
          {
            "content": "54: - The `http_config.enable_http2` in `remote_write` items default has been\n55:   changed to `false`. In Prometheus v2 the remote write http client would\n56:   default to use http2. In order to parallelize multiple remote write queues\n57: ",
            "endLine": 57,
            "startLine": 54
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/migration.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/docs/migration.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2126: \n2127: * [BUGFIX] Fix disappearing Alertmanager targets in Alertmanager discovery.\n2128: * [BUGFIX] Fix panic with remote_write on ARMv7.\n2129: ",
            "endLine": 2129,
            "startLine": 2126
          },
          {
            "content": "1227: \n1228: In the next minor release (2.26), Prometheus will use the Alertmanager API v2.\n1229: It will be done by defaulting `alertmanager_config.api_version` to `v2`.\n1230: Alertmanager API v2 was released in Alertmanager v0.16.0 (released in January\n1231: 2019).\n1232: ",
            "endLine": 1232,
            "startLine": 1227
          },
          {
            "content": "2464: * [ENHANCEMENT] Quarantine series file if data corruption is encountered (instead of crashing)\n2465: * [ENHANCEMENT] Validate Alertmanager URL\n2466: * [ENHANCEMENT] Use UTC for build timestamp\n2467: ",
            "endLine": 2467,
            "startLine": 2464
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4",
        "type": "file",

`yaml:\\\"send_interval\\\"`\\n1518: \\t// Maximum number of samples per send.\\n1519: \",\"endLine\":1519,\"startLine\":1510},{\"content\":\"372: \\t// We want to set c to the defaults and then \\t// Option used by downstream scraper users like OpenTelemetry Collector\\n77: \\t// to help lookup metric metadata. Should be false for Prometheus.\\n78: \\tPassMetadataInContext bool\\n79: \\t// Option to enable appending of scraped Metadata to the TSDB/other appenders. Individual appenders\\n80: \\t// can decide what to do with metadata, but for practical purposes this flag exists so that metadata\\n81: \\t// can be written to the WAL and thus read for remote write.\\n82: \\t// TODO: implement some form of metadata storage\\n83: \\tAppendMetadata bool\\n84: \\t// Option to increase the interval used by scrape manager to throttle target groups updates.\\n85: \\tDiscoveryReloadInterval model.Duration\\n86: \\t// Option to enable the ingestion of the created timestamp as a synthetic zero sample.\\n87: \",\"endLine\":87,\"startLine\":76},{\"content\":\"73: // Options are the configuration parameters to the scrape manager.\\n74: type Options struct {\\n75: \\tExtraMetrics bool\\n76: \",\"endLine\":76,\"startLine\":73},{\"content\":\"103: // when receiving new target groups from the discovery manager.\\n104: type Manager struct {\\n105: \\topts      *Options\\n106:             "content": "52: //     for the \"classic\" representation of complex metrics like histograms. The\n53: //     Metadata.Name follows the __name__ semantics. See Name for details.\n54: //   - Original metadata.Metadata entries. However, not all fields in that metadata\n55: //     are \"identifiable\", notably the help field, plus metadata does not contain Name.\n56: type Metadata struct {\n57: \t// Name represents the final metric name for a Prometheus series.\n58: ",
            "endLine": 58,
            "startLine": 52
          },
          {
            "content": "141: \n142: // IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelsScratchBuilder is a wrapper over labels scratch builder\n143: // that ignores label additions that would collide with non-empty Overwrite Metadata fields.\n144: type IgnoreOverriddenMetadataLabelsScratchBuilder struct {\n145: \t*labels.ScratchBuilder\n146: ",
            "endLine": 146,
            "startLine": 141
          },
          {
            "content": "43: // Historically, similar information was encoded in the labels.MetricName (suffixes)\n44: // and in the separate metadata.Metadata structures. However, with the\n45: // type-and-unit-label feature (PROM-39), this information can be now stored directly\n46: // in the special schema metadata labels, which offers better reliability (e.g. atomicity),\n47: // compatibility and, in many cases, efficiency.\n48: ",
            "endLine": 48,
            "startLine": 43
          }
        ],
        "file": "schema/labels.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4/-/blob/schema/labels.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "43: \n44: // MetadataWatcher watches the Scrape Manager for a given WriteMetadataTo.\n45: type MetadataWatcher struct {\n46: \tname   string\n47: ",
            "endLine": 47,
            "startLine": 43
          },
          {
            "content": "132: \n133: \t// We create a set of the metadata to help deduplicating based on the attributes of a\n134: \t// scrape.MetricMetadata. In this case, a combination of metric name, help, type, and unit.\n135: \tmetadataSet := map[scrape.MetricMetadata]struct{}{}\n136: \tmetadata := []scrape.MetricMetadata{}\n137: \tfor _, tset := range mw.manager.TargetsActive() {\n138: ",
            "endLine": 138,
            "startLine": 132
          },
          {
            "content": "138: \t\tfor _, target := range tset {\n139: \t\t\tfor _, entry := range target.ListMetadata() {\n140: \t\t\t\tif _, ok := metadataSet[entry]; !ok {\n141: \t\t\t\t\tmetadata = append(metadata, entry)\n142: \t\t\t\t\tmetadataSet[entry] = struct{}{}\n143: \t\t\t\t}\n144: ",
            "endLine": 144,
            "startLine": 138
          }
        ],

            "content": "76: \t// Option used by downstream scraper users like OpenTelemetry Collector\n77: \t// to help lookup metric metadata. Should be false for Prometheus.\n78: \tPassMetadataInContext bool\n79: \t// Option to enable appending of scraped Metadata to the TSDB/other appenders. Individual appenders\n80: \t// can decide what to do with metadata, but for practical purposes this flag exists so that metadata\n81: \t// can be written to the WAL and thus read for remote write.\n82: \t// TODO: implement some form of metadata storage\n83: \tAppendMetadata bool\n84: \t// Option to increase the interval used by scrape manager to throttle target groups updates.\n85: \tDiscoveryReloadInterval model.Duration\n86: \t// Option to enable the ingestion of the created timestamp as a synthetic zero sample.\n87: ",
            "endLine": 87,
            "startLine": 76
          },

    "content": "// Copyright 2016 The Prometheus Authors\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage v1\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"reflect\"\n\t\"runtime\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tjsoniter \"github.com/json-iterator/go\"\n\t\"github.com/oklog/ulid/v2\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n\tconfig_util \"github.com/prometheus/common/config\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/common/model\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/common/route\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/exemplar\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/metadata\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/timestamp\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/prompb\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/parser\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/promql/promqltest\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/rules\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/scrape\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage/remote\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/tsdb\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/stats\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/teststorage\"\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/testutil\"\n)\n\nfunc testEngine(t *testing.T) *promql.Engine {\n\tt.Helper()\n\treturn promqltest.NewTestEngineWithOpts(t, promql.EngineOpts{\n\t\tLogger:                   nil,\n\t\tReg:                      nil,\n\t\tMaxSamples:               10000,\n\t\tTimeout:                  100 * time.Second,\n\t\tNoStepSubqueryIntervalFn: func(int64) int64 { return 60 * 1000 },\n\t\tEnableAtModifier:         true,\n\t\tEnableNegativeOffset:     true,\n\t\tEnablePerStepStats:       true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// testMetaStore satisfies the scrape.MetricMetadataStore interface.\n// It is used to inject specific metadata as part of a test case.\ntype testMetaStore struct {\n\tMetadata []scrape.MetricMetadata\n}\n\nfunc (s *testMetaStore) ListMetadata() []scrape.MetricMetadata {\n\treturn s.Metadata\n}\n\nfunc (s *testMetaStore) GetMetadata(metric string) (scrape.MetricMetadata, bool) {\n\tfor _, m := range s.Metadata {\n\t\tif metric == m.MetricFamily {\n\t\t\treturn m, true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn scrape.MetricMetadata{}, false\n}\n\nfunc (*testMetaStore) SizeMetadata() int   { return 0 }\nfunc (*testMetaStore) LengthMetadata() int { return 0 }\n\n// testTargetRetriever represents a list of targets to scrape.\n// It is used to represent targets as part of test cases.",

    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 4804
  },
  "type": "text"
}

63. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 372
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "func (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\n\twrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\t\thf := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n\t\t\tresult := setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(f(r))\n\t\t\tif result.finalizer != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdefer result.finalizer()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif result.err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapi.respondError(w, result.err, result.data)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.data != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapi.respond(w, r, result.data, result.warnings, r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn api.ready(httputil.CompressionHandler{\n\t\t\tHandler: hf,\n\t\t}.ServeHTTP)\n\t}\n\n\twrapAgent := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\t\treturn wrap(func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\t\t\tif api.isAgent {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, errors.New(\"unavailable with Prometheus Agent\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn f(r)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tr.Options(\"/*path\", wrap(api.options))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n\tr.Post(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n\tr.Get(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n\tr.Post(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n\tr.Get(\"/query_exemplars\", wrapAgent(api.queryExemplars))\n\tr.Post(\"/query_exemplars\", wrapAgent(api.queryExemplars))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/format_query\", wrapAgent(api.formatQuery))\n\tr.Post(\"/format_query\", wrapAgent(api.formatQuery))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/parse_query\", wrapAgent(api.parseQuery))\n\tr.Post(\"/parse_query\", wrapAgent(api.parseQuery))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/labels\", wrapAgent(api.labelNames))\n\tr.Post(\"/labels\", wrapAgent(api.labelNames))\n\tr.Get(\"/label/:name/values\", wrapAgent(api.labelValues))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/series\", wrapAgent(api.series))\n\tr.Post(\"/series\", wrapAgent(api.series))\n\tr.Del(\"/series\", wrapAgent(api.dropSeries))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/scrape_pools\", wrap(api.scrapePools))\n\tr.Get(\"/targets\", wrap(api.targets))\n\tr.Get(\"/targets/metadata\", wrap(api.targetMetadata))\n\tr.Get(\"/alertmanagers\", wrapAgent(api.alertmanagers))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/metadata\", wrap(api.metricMetadata))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/status/config\", wrap(api.serveConfig))\n\tr.Get(\"/status/runtimeinfo\", wrap(api.serveRuntimeInfo))\n\tr.Get(\"/status/buildinfo\", wrap(api.serveBuildInfo))\n\tr.Get(\"/status/flags\", wrap(api.serveFlags))\n\tr.Get(\"/status/tsdb\", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBStatus))\n\tr.Get(\"/status/tsdb/blocks\", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBBlocks))\n\tr.Get(\"/status/walreplay\", api.serveWALReplayStatus)\n\tr.Get(\"/notifications\", api.notifications)\n\tr.Get(\"/notifications/live\", api.notificationsSSE)\n\tr.Post(\"/read\", api.ready(api.remoteRead))\n\tr.Post(\"/write\", api.ready(api.remoteWrite))\n\tr.Post(\"/otlp/v1/metrics\", api.ready(api.otlpWrite))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/alerts\", wrapAgent(api.alerts))\n\tr.Get(\"/rules\", wrapAgent(api.rules))\n\n\t// Admin APIs\n\tr.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/delete_series\", wrapAgent(api.deleteSeries))\n\tr.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones\", wrapAgent(api.cleanTombstones))\n\tr.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/snapshot\", wrapAgent(api.snapshot))\n\n\tr.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/delete_series\", wrapAgent(api.deleteSeries))\n\tr.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones\", wrapAgent(api.cleanTombstones))\n\tr.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/snapshot\", wrapAgent(api.snapshot))\n}\n\ntype QueryData struct {\n\tResultType parser.ValueType `json:\"resultType\"`\n\tResult     parser.Value     `json:\"result\"`\n\tStats      stats.QueryStats `json:\"stats,omitempty\"`\n}\n\nfunc invalidParamError(err error, parameter string) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{\n\t\terrorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid parameter %q: %w\", parameter, err),\n\t}, nil, nil}\n}",
    "filePath": "/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 372,
    "totalLines": 2221
  },
  "type": "text"
}

66. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright 2024 The Prometheus Authors\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage v1\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels\"\n)\n\n// Matcher represents a label matcher.\ntype Matcher struct {\n\tName    string `json:\"name\"`\n\tValue   string `json:\"value\"`\n\tIsRegex bool   `json:\"isRegex\"`\n}\n\n// Silence represents a silence for suppressing alerts.\ntype Silence struct {\n\tID        string     `json:\"id,omitempty\"`\n\tMatchers  []Matcher  `json:\"matchers\"`\n\tStartsAt  *time.Time `json:\"startsAt,omitempty\"`\n\tEndsAt    *time.Time `json:\"endsAt\"`\n\tCreatedBy string     `json:\"createdBy,omitempty\"`\n\tComment   string     `json:\"comment\"`\n}\n\n// SilenceResponse represents the response from creating a silence.\ntype SilenceResponse struct {\n\tID string `json:\"id\"`\n}\n\n// BulkSilenceResponse represents the response from creating multiple silences.\ntype BulkSilenceResponse struct {\n\tIDs []string `json:\"ids\"`\n}\n\n// BulkSilenceError represents an error for a specific silence in a bulk request.\ntype BulkSilenceError struct {\n\tIndex   int    `json:\"index\"`\n\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n}\n\n// SilenceStore interface for managing silences.\ntype SilenceStore interface {\n\tCreate(s *Silence) (string, error)\n\tGet(id string) (*Silence, error)\n\tDelete(id string) error\n\tList() []*Silence\n}\n\n// InMemorySilenceStore is a simple in-memory store for silences.\ntype InMemorySilenceStore struct {\n\tsilences map[string]*Silence\n\tcounter  int\n}\n\n// NewInMemorySilenceStore creates a new in-memory silence store.\nfunc NewInMemorySilenceStore() *InMemorySilenceStore {\n\treturn &InMemorySilenceStore{\n\t\tsilences: make(map[string]*Silence),\n\t\tcounter:  0,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Create stores a silence and returns its ID.\nfunc (s *InMemorySilenceStore) Create(silence *Silence) (string, error) {\n\tif silence == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", errors.New(\"silence cannot be nil\")\n\t}\n\n\ts.counter++\n\tid := fmt.Sprintf(\"silence_%d\", s.counter)\n\tsilence.ID = id\n\ts.silences[id] = silence\n\treturn id, nil\n}\n\n// Get retrieves a silence by ID.\nfunc (s *InMemorySilenceStore) Get(id string) (*Silence, error) {\n\tsilence, exists := s.silences[id]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"silence not found: %s\", id)\n\t}\n\treturn silence, nil\n}\n\n// Delete removes a silence by ID.\nfunc (s *InMemorySilenceStore) Delete(id string) error {\n\tif _, exists := s.silences[id]; !exists {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"silence not found: %s\", id)\n\t}\n\tdelete(s.silences, id)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// List returns all silences.\nfunc (s *InMemorySilenceStore) List() []*Silence {\n\tresult := make([]*Silence, 0, len(s.silences))\n\tfor _, silence := range s.silences {\n\t\tresult = append(result, silence)\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// validateSilence performs validation on a silence.\nfunc validateSilence(s *Silence) error {\n\tif s == nil {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"silence cannot be nil\")\n\t}\n\n\tif len(s.Matchers) == 0 {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"at least one matcher is required\")\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, m := range s.Matchers {\n\t\tif m.Name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"matcher %d: name is required\", i)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif m.Value == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"matcher %d: value is required\", i)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Validate label name\n\t\tif !labels.IsValidLabelName(labels.LabelName(m.Name)) {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"matcher %d: invalid label name %q\", i, m.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif s.EndsAt == nil {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"endsAt is required\")\n\t}\n\n\tif s.StartsAt != nil && s.EndsAt != nil && s.StartsAt.After(*s.EndsAt) {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"startsAt must be before endsAt\")\n\t}\n\n\tif s.Comment == \"\" {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"comment is required\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// postSilences handles POST requests for creating a single silence.\nfunc (api *API) postSilences(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"unable to read body\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tvar silence Silence\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(body, &silence); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid JSON: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tif err := validateSilence(&silence); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the silence store from API (will be injected in constructor)\n\tsilenceStore := api.silenceStore\n\tif silenceStore == nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, errors.New(\"silence store not configured\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tid, err := silenceStore.Create(&silence)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&SilenceResponse{ID: id}, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// postSilencesBulk handles POST requests for creating multiple silences atomically.\nfunc (api *API) postSilencesBulk(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"unable to read body\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tvar silences []Silence\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(body, &silences); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid JSON: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(silences) == 0 {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{&BulkSilenceResponse{IDs: []string{}}, nil, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate all silences before creating any (atomic semantics)\n\tvalidationErrors := make([]BulkSilenceError, 0)\n\tfor i, silence := range silences {\n\t\tif err := validateSilence(&silence); err != nil {\n\t\t\tvalidationErrors = append(validationErrors, BulkSilenceError{\n\t\t\t\tIndex:   i,\n\t\t\t\tMessage: err.Error(),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If there are validation errors, return them all\n\tif len(validationErrors) > 0 {\n\t\terrorData := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"errors\": validationErrors,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{errorData, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"validation failed for one or more silences\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tsilenceStore := api.silenceStore\n\tif silenceStore == nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, errors.New(\"silence store not configured\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create all silences\n\tids := make([]string, 0, len(silences))\n\tfor _, silence := range silences {\n\t\ts := silence // Create a copy for the store\n\t\tid, err := silenceStore.Create(&s)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create silence: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t\tids = append(ids, id)\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&BulkSilenceResponse{IDs: ids}, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/web/api/v1/silences.go"
}

s.StartsAt.After(*s.EndsAt) {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"startsAt must be before endsAt\")\n\t}\n\n\tif s.Comment == \"\" {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"comment is required\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// postSilences handles POST requests for creating a single silence.\nfunc (api *API) postSilences(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"unable to read body\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tvar silence Silence\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(body, &silence); err != nil {\n\t\treturn Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "type API struct {\n\tQueryable         storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable\n\tQueryEngine       promql.QueryEngine\n\tExemplarQueryable storage.ExemplarQueryable\n\n\tscrapePoolsRetriever  func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever\n\ttargetRetriever       func(context.Context) TargetRetriever\n\talertmanagerRetriever func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever\n\trulesRetriever        func(context.Context) RulesRetriever\n\tnow                   func() time.Time\n\tconfig                func() config.Config\n\tflagsMap              map[string]string\n\tready                 func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc\n\tglobalURLOptions      GlobalURLOptions\n\n\tdb                  TSDBAdminStats\n\tdbDir               string\n\tenableAdmin         bool\n\tlogger              *slog.Logger\n\tCORSOrigin          *regexp.Regexp\n\tbuildInfo           *PrometheusVersion\n\truntimeInfo         func() (RuntimeInfo, error)\n\tgatherer            prometheus.Gatherer\n\tisAgent             bool\n\tstatsRenderer       StatsRenderer\n\tnotificationsGetter func() []notifications.Notification\n\tnotificationsSub    func() (<-chan notifications.Notification, func(), bool)\n\t// Allows customizing the default mapping\n\toverrideErrorCode OverrideErrorCode\n\n\tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\n\tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\n\totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\n\n\tcodecs []Codec\n}\n\n// NewAPI returns an initialized API type.\nfunc NewAPI(\n\tqe promql.QueryEngine,\n\tq storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable,\n\tap storage.Appendable,\n\teq storage.ExemplarQueryable,\n\tspsr func(context.Context) ScrapePoolsRetriever,\n\ttr func(context.Context) TargetRetriever,\n\tar func(context.Context) AlertmanagerRetriever,\n\tconfigFunc func() config.Config,\n\tflagsMap map[string]string,\n\tglobalURLOptions GlobalURLOptions,\n\treadyFunc func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc,\n\tdb TSDBAdminStats,\n\tdbDir string,\n\tenableAdmin bool,\n\tlogger *slog.Logger,\n\trr func(context.Context) RulesRetriever,\n\tremoteReadSampleLimit int,\n\tremoteReadConcurrencyLimit int,\n\tremoteReadMaxBytesInFrame int,\n\tisAgent bool,\n\tcorsOrigin *regexp.Regexp,",
    "filePath": "/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go",

68. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go",
  "new_string": "\tremoteWriteHandler http.Handler\n\tremoteReadHandler  http.Handler\n\totlpWriteHandler   http.Handler\n\n\tsilenceStore SilenceStore\n\n\tcodecs []Codec\n}",
